ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Hearing Re Closing Of Estate.
Off Calendar Feb-28-2018 Off Calendar

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Feb-05-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: John Golden for Anthony Oltranti.

Mr. Golden presented oral argument to support extraordinary fees stating that this was a contested probate, that there was litigation/a will contest and that the Contestants challenged everything. Mr. Golden also stated that $32,000.00 was preparation of estate taxes, that $8,600.00 was for the petition to clear title, that the decedent had a 50% ownership in a property/vineyard, that some assets were in Italy and that there were significant non-probate assets that were litigated. The Petition for Final Distribution on Waiver of Account, etc. is taken under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: M. Nuque. Not reported.

Off Calendar Aug-29-2016 Off Calendar

Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Aug-18-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kendal Fletcher for petitioner Anthony Oltranti; Terrence Mayo for objectors Maria Lembi, Barbara La Gioia and Guilia Gambogi.

The Court stated his understanding that the funding of the $500,000 settlement payment is to come from the funds of Mr. Oltranti, and not from the estate. Mr. Mayo confirmed that this is correct. The Court stated that he needed a revised order stating that approval of the settlement did not constitute a finding that Mt. Oltranti was entitled to reimbursement from the estate for making the settlement payment. Ms. Fletcher agreed to provide the revised order. The Court granted the petition (subject to receipt and review of the order).
Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Chan. (Not reported)

Petition To Approve Settlement Agreement
Off Calendar Jul-19-2016 Continued To Aug-16-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Kendall Fletcher appearing for Steven Braccini for (petitioner) Anthony Oltranti.

The Court stated that this is a petition to approve a settlement agreement, that no one is here to object, that the court has questions, that the settlement agreement calls for payment of $500,000,00 within several days of the approval, that is it not clear if anyone is in a position to make the payment, that they may need a petition for partial distribution or to wait for the general petition to be heard in August and that it is not sure the creditors have been notified which could create a problem. Ms. Fletcher stated that this is a 7 million dollar estate, that there is minimal debt, that there have been no substantial creditors ascertained, that there is a cash account but is not sure that it contains the $500,000.00 and that they may need to get a loan. The Court stated that the settlement agreement calls for payment in 5 days and that it does not want to create a breach of the settlement agreement. Ms. Fletcher stated that they have worked closely with opposing counsel and that they know what the assets are. The Court suggested that this be continued, that it is not sure if counsel needs to amend the settlement agreement or this petition, that it is not proposing a solution, that as it is proposed the court is not in a position to sign and that if the petition is amended it will need to be re-noticed, that there is also an issue with the provision stating that the accounts totaling $1,176,000.00 arguably belonging to the estate, that it is not sure what this means. Ms. Fletcher stated that it will be clarified in the amendment, that the Special Administrator's letters expired in May, that they were expecting an order extending to be issued by Judge Cheng but confirmed that no order had been submitted to him. The Court stated that they are to file an ex parte petition to extend or renew the letters. The Petition to Approve Settlement Agreement was continued to August 16, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar May-18-2016 Continued To Aug-29-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
Off Calendar May-18-2016 Continued To Aug-29-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Terence O'Connell Mayo and Mary Mayo for Barbara La Gioia-Kotlarz (Petitioner). Steven Braccini for and with Anthony Oltranti (Objector).

The Court asked if trial and mediation dates need to be set.

Ms. Mayo stated that Paul Oltranti is wiling to serve, that he is the first choice in the 2009 will to serve, that Barbara is willing to set aside to allow Paul to file his own petition, that they have filed 2 proofs of subscribing witnesses and hopes that they can be used for Paul's petition.

Mr. Braccini reported that no discovery has been conducted yet, request a 90 days continuance, requested that nothing substantive be ordered, requested to extend the special administrators letters and that it is not necessary for Barbara to step aside since an executor has not been appointed. Ms. Mayo argued that Paul should discuss this with his siblings, that there is no need to extend the letters, that they have had two weeks and could have filed the 706, that her client is stepping aside and will not participate in the litigation.

Mr. Braccini argued that Paul was notified of the proceedings and that he could have stepped forward earlier, in agreement with his brother serving, that a 706 can not be done in one week and that it is due in July, that they are getting appraisals and that they filed original will. Ms. Mayo responded that she is not willing to step in and litigate, that Barbara only has a nominal interest, that Paul can not nominate Tony, that their is a conflict of interest and they would need an independent person appointed for the 2009 will.

Mr. Mayo stated that they are on the short track in terms of discovery, that a number of depositions will be taken and that it would be appropriate to meet with mediator Pang Ly. Ms. Mayo agreed to the recommendation of Pang Ly. The Court stated that it has no problem with mandatory settlement and asked about the time counsel needed.

Mr. Braccini stated that he is concerned that discovery would not be complete by July and that they are being overly optimistic. Ms. Mayo objected to the extension of the letters, that she does not trust them, that she is only willing to continue the letter for up to three weeks if they get certain documents. Mr. Mayo clarified that the bond will remain in place.

The Court stated that it will take the proposal under submission, that it will give it's ruling within one week and that parties are to file whatever they feel necessary.
The Court set a mediation deadline of August 19, 2016 and stated that if they can not find a private mediator then the Court will refer them to a mandatory settlement conference. The competing petition were continued August 29, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Nancy Wong/ Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. Not Reported. Court Reporter: Donielle Del Carlo. CSR: 10476. Telephone:(415)357-4300.
